note: Shared tuning catalogue. strings[] is ordered low -> high and may be any length (4-8+), so 7/8-string guitars, bass and ukulele drop in without schema changes. Focus is ALTERNATE tunings: songs played in plain E standard are deliberately not catalogued. confidence=high means cross-checked against multiple sources; medium means commonly cited but worth confirming by ear. Where sources genuinely disagree, both readings are offered as variants. Entries sourced from the Songsterr catalogue are marked confidence=medium and carry sources=['Songsterr catalogue API']; they reflect a transcriber's tuning and are not verified against the recording.

tunings.name: E Standard
tunings.desc: Standard tuning. Most songs live here.

tunings.name: E♭ Standard
tunings.desc: Half step down. Hendrix, Guns N' Roses, lots of metal.

tunings.name: D Standard
tunings.desc: Whole step down. Thicker, heavier body.

tunings.name: C# Standard
tunings.desc: Minor third down. Dark and low.

tunings.name: C Standard
tunings.desc: Two whole steps down. Heavy low end.

tunings.name: Drop D
tunings.desc: Low E dropped to D. One-finger power chords.

tunings.name: Drop C
tunings.desc: D standard with the 6th dropped to C.

tunings.name: Drop B
tunings.desc: Very low and heavy. Slipknot Vol. 3 territory.

tunings.name: DADGAD
tunings.desc: Modal, droning, open. Celtic and Zeppelin.

tunings.name: Open D
tunings.desc: Strums an open D major. Slide favourite.

tunings.name: Open E
tunings.desc: Open D shapes at standard pitch. Bottleneck blues.

tunings.name: Open G
tunings.desc: Keith Richards territory (he drops the 6th string entirely).

tunings.name: Drop C#
tunings.desc: E♭ standard with the 6th string dropped to C#.

tunings.name: Drop A
tunings.desc: Lower than Drop B. Slipknot go here for a handful of songs.

variants.label: Studio (Vol. 3)
variants.note: Vol. 3: (The Subliminal Verses) was tracked in Drop B. Many tabs are written in E standard for convenience — the record is not.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Open E with a capo at fret 3 (sounds as open G). Played with a slide/bottleneck.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab header (E B E G# B E)
songs.album: Folk Songs of the American Longhair

variants.label: Studio (Nevermind)
variants.note: Original recording is a whole step down, D standard. No capo.

variants.label: Live
variants.note: Live, Nirvana usually tuned only a half step down. Played identically.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Page's signature DADGAD drone. The riff relies on the open D/A strings ringing.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop D. The main riff is built on the dropped 6th string.

variants.label: Keith Richards (5-string)
variants.note: Open G on FIVE strings: Keith removes the low 6th entirely. On a normal six-string, tune to open G and simply ignore the 6th string. Intentionally not identical to the six-string open-g entry.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Standard tuning — no retune needed.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Hendrix almost always tuned a half step down.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop C. Verify by ear against the record — SOAD used several low tunings across albums.

variants.label: Studio (Vol. 3)
variants.note: Drop B. Same Vol. 3 sessions as Vermilion Pt. 2.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not. Inferred from the album's usual tuning; not individually verified.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab headers list B F# B E G# C# for this era

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop A, not Drop B. Slipknot drop below their usual Drop B for this one. Transposed Drop B arrangements of it exist, but the record is Drop A.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab header A E A D F# B

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not. Inferred from the album's usual tuning; not individually verified.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab headers list B F# B E G# C# for this era

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not. Inferred from the album's usual tuning; not individually verified.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab headers list B F# B E G# C# for this era

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop B. Tabs are often written in E standard for convenience; the record is not.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab headers list B F# B E G# C# for this era

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: D standard. Written and demoed in E standard, then dropped a whole step on Bob Rock's suggestion.

songs.song: The Thing That Should Not Be

variants.label: D standard (most cited)
variants.note: Sources disagree: many transcriptions say D standard, older ones say Drop D, and Kirk Hammett has been quoted saying C#. Check by ear against the record.

variants.label: Drop D (alternate)
variants.note: Some published tabs use Drop D instead.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: D standard.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: D standard.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: D standard.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: E♭ standard — the odd one out on an album that is mostly E standard.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Load is E♭ standard throughout except this track, which drops the 6th string to C#.

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop D.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab header D A D G B E

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop D.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab header D A D G B E

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop D.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab header D A D G B E

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop D.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab header D A D G B E

variants.label: Drop D
variants.note: Playable either way; several tabbers note the band plays it in Drop D.

variants.label: E standard
variants.note: The riff sits on the low strings and also works unmodified in standard tuning.

sources: Bass tab notes the band plays Drop D

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop C — the heavier low tuning of Rammstein's later records.

sources: Ultimate-Guitar tab header C G C F A D

variants.label: Studio
variants.note: Drop C — the heavier low tuning of Rammstein's later records.
